items.title: RM1.1b investment probe: MACC seeks AGC help to get statements from Taiwan, UK in case linked to Arm Holdings
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is seeking mutual legal assistance to obtain statements from several individuals in Taiwan and the United Kingdom (UK) to assist in the investigation into the RM1.1 billion investment case. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said his party had contacted the Attorney General’s Chambers to seek assistance following the investigation which also involved the jurisdictions of the two countries involved. So the investigation may take time,” he told Bernama after a working visit to the Selangor MACC office here today.

items.title: Raul Castro: The other leader of Cuba's revolution
items.summary: RAUL Castro, the younger brother of Fidel Castro and the last surviving cast member of the Cuban revolution, relinquished power in 2021 but remains an influential figure in national politics. The revolutionary leader still follows Washington-Havana negotiations "very closely" and takes part in "decision-making," his daughter Mariela Castro told AFP in April. When the revolution triumphed in January 1959, Raul Castro became second-in-command to Fidel.

items.title: Anwar calls on Malaysian envoys to boost economic diplomacy, attract quality investments
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim wants Malaysian diplomats to continue strengthening the nation’s economic diplomacy capabilities, particularly in attracting trade, high-quality investments and exploring strategic cooperation opportunities capable of empowering sustainable national economic growth. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, emphasised the matter when receiving courtesy calls from 13 Malaysian Heads of Diplomatic Missions who will soon be serving in various strategic regions around the world yesterday. Anwar said Malaysia would continue to uphold the principles of moderation, stability, good governance as well as a diplomacy approach grounded in wisdom and universal humanitarian values in carrying the nation’s voice on the world stage.

items.title: Two men killed in suspected gold mining incident
items.summary: Jeli Fire and Rescue Station chief Adni Ibrahim said they received an emergency call reporting that victims were buried under the soil, after which nine personnel rushed to the scene to conduct a search and rescue operation. "However, the rescue operation faced difficulties as the fire engine could not enter the plantation area due to the narrow and challenging path. "The search operation began at 9.20pm, and it was found that two victims were buried due to the collapse of a two-metre-high earth embankment near the stream," he said.

items.title: UDA launches Residensi Mutiara to boost homeownership in Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UDA Holdings Bhd, an agency under the Ministry of Entrepreneur and Cooperative Development (KUSKOP), continues to strengthen property ownership opportunities, especially in Johor, with the launch of its latest housing development, Mutiara Residence. Chief executive officer Datuk Sri Ahmad Fuaad Kenali said the Mutiara Residence project reflects UDA's continuous efforts in developing homes that meet current needs while enabling more Malaysians to own a home at competitive prices. He said Mutiara Residence also incorporates sustainability features aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals.

items.title: Motorcyclist killed in collision with excavator
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A motorcyclist was killed after his vehicle crashed into an excavator, trapping him underneath the heavy machinery here at midnight. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations division assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said they received an emergency call at 12.06am. "The operation involved five personnel from the Shah Alam Fire and Rescue Station using a Fire Rescue Tender vehicle," he said.

items.title: HSS Engineers Q1 profit more than doubles on stronger margins despite lower revenue
items.summary: In terms of segmental performance, HSS Engineers said project management remained the company's largest revenue contributor at RM 21.3 million, equivalent to 46.2 per cent of total revenue. The company said engineering design contributed RM11.7 million, while construction supervision recorded RM10.6 million. HSS Engineers' executive vice chairman, Tan Sri Kuna Sittampalam, said the company remained well-positioned to benefit from Malaysia's accelerating infrastructure development cycle.

items.title: Too late: Housewife's Semak Mule check confirms RM749,000 scam
items.summary: Kuala Terengganu police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the 43-year-old woman's ordeal began in July last year when she received calls from two people claiming to be an insurance official and a police officer. The callers allegedly told her she was involved in a money laundering case linked to her bank account. "She was also told to transfer funds into an account provided by the syndicate, purportedly for a Bank Negara Malaysia investigation," Azli said.

items.title: Bersama's arrival could further divide Malays, says Wan Rosdy
items.summary: KUANTAN: The entry of Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) into the Malaysian political landscape will further divide the Malays, says Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail. The Pahang Menteri Besar said he believes the Malay support base is becoming increasingly fragmented with the emergence of new political parties. Meanwhile, commenting on his meeting with Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister (NRES) Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup in Putrajaya on Tuesday, Wan Rosdy said they had touched on the issue of river sand exports.

items.title: TikTok, YouTube lag on UK child safety as rivals act, regulator says
items.summary: LONDON: TikTok and Alphabet's YouTube have failed to set out meaningful steps to protect British children from harmful online content, media regulator Ofcom said on Thursday, citing data showing widespread exposure on their platforms. Ofcom said neither company had made significant new commitments to make recommendation feeds safer, despite evidence that these feeds are the main route through which children encounter harm. Ofcom said TikTok and YouTube maintained their existing systems were sufficient, but the regulator said its evidence suggested their feeds "are still not safe enough."

items.title: Nexstar says it is losing millions as it seeks quick review of order halting Tegna merger
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Nexstar Media Group asked a US appeals court late on Wednesday to expedite a review of a lower-court order that has halted its merger with rival broadcaster Tegna, saying the delay has lost tens of millions of dollars it can never recover. The deal would create the largest broadcast ⁠station group in the United States, reaching 80 per cent of households. Court of Appeals to schedule oral arguments for August on the deal.

items.title: Fire races through police motorcycle bay, 4 bikes destroyed
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH: Four traffic police motorcycles were destroyed in a fire at the district police headquarters early today. The 2.27am incident also destroyed a privately-owned motorcycle and caused minor damage to part of the police headquarters building. Pasir Puteh Fire and Rescue Station chief Azhar Elmi Mustoffar said the motorcycles were completely destroyed.

items.title: Samsung Electronics' shares jump after tentative wage deal suspends strike
items.summary: SEOUL: Samsung Electronics' shares rose as much as 6.5 per cent in morning trade on Thursday after the tech giant and its South Korean union reached a tentative pay deal, potentially averting a strike that had threatened to hit the economy and undermine global chip supply chains. The union said the planned 18-day strike by nearly 48,000 members would be suspended while the tentative agreement is put to a vote between May 22 and 27. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Bond yields, oil fall amid hopes for Iran deal; stocks end up with chip shares
items.summary: The US dollar fell from a six-week high, while US crude fell US$5.89 to settle at US$98.26 a barrel. Brent dropped US$6.26 to settle at US$105.02. The yield on benchmark US 10-year notes fell 9.4 basis points to 4.576 per cent, from 4.669 per cent late on Tuesday.

items.title: MAG expects jet fuel prices to hover at US$120-US$130 over the next one year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) expects jet fuel prices to hover between US$120 and US$130 per barrel over the next one year, based on current forward market indications, as airlines continue navigating geopolitical tensions and fuel supply disruptions affecting the aviation industry. MAG president and group chief executive officer Captain Nasaruddin A. Bakar said fuel prices were hovering around US$100 per barrel before geopolitical tensions pushed prices as high as US$240 per barrel, underscoring continued volatility facing airlines globally. Nasaruddin said geopolitical tensions involving Iran have affected the aviation sector not only through fuel costs but also fuel supply concerns, creating operational challenges for airlines globally.

items.title: Former DOJ lawyer charged with theft of unreleased report on Trump documents case
items.summary: BOSTON: A former US Department of Justice attorney has been charged with emailing herself copies of an unreleased volume of Special Counsel Jack Smith's report concerning the now-dismissed criminal case accusing President Trump of retaining classified documents after his first term. Trump was accused in the case pursued by Smith of illegally storing documents related to US national defence, including the American nuclear programme, at his Mar-a-Lago social club and obstructing US government efforts to retrieve the material. Florida-based US District Judge Aileen Cannon, a Trump appointee, dismissed the indictment in 2024, finding that Smith had not been lawfully appointed by the Justice Department during Democratic President Joe Biden's administration.

items.title: Why being transparent matters
items.summary: Transparency is one of the most important foundations of a successful and sustainable business. While some organisations may fear that transparency exposes weaknesses, the reality is that transparent businesses often build stronger trust, better reputations, and greater long-term resilience. Strengthens trust One of the primary reasons transparency matters in business is because it builds trust.

items.title: SkyeChip tops main market IPOs [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: SkyeChip Bhd, which was listed on Wednesday, has emerged as the strongest performing initial public offering (IPO) on Bursa Malaysia's Main Market so far this year. It closed 151.14 per cent higher, or RM1.33 above its IPO price, at RM2.21, with trading volume of 292.88 million shares, valuing the company at a market capitalisation of RM3.96 billion. SkyeChip is the 26th company to debut on Bursa Malaysia this year.

items.title: Malaysia's trade surges 28.6pct to record RM336.73bil in April [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's trade momentum, which hit its fastest growth since September 2022, can be sustained in the near term but remains vulnerable to external shocks, economists said. The trade rose 28.6 per cent year-on-year to a record RM336.73 billion in April 2026, driven mainly by strong exports of electrical and electronic products, said Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry. Imports rose 20 per cent to RM153.99 billion, extending Malaysia's trade surplus streak to 72 consecutive months since May 2020.

items.title: Return to sender: Port Klang authorities to ship out 80 more e-waste containers
items.summary: Port Klang Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said 55 containers had already been successfully shipped out under the ongoing enforcement and repatriation exercise. Nik Ezanee said the "repatriation" exercise, coordinated through the Port Klang Joint Task Force (JTF), had so far progressed smoothly despite minor operational issues. He said the authorities were currently handling the repatriation exercise in two phases.

items.title: Trump nominee vows to press Seoul on its US$350 billion pledge
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump's nominee to be the US ambassador to South Korea vowed on Wednesday to press South Korea to learn where the US$350 billion Seoul has pledged to invest in the United States will come from. Former California congresswoman Michelle Steel also told her Senate confirmation hearing that US firms in South Korea deserved the same market access Korean companies enjoy in the United States. Last year Washington and Seoul agreed the broad outlines of an agreement on trade and investment under which South Korea committed to invest US$350 billion in the United States in return for more favourable tariff terms.

items.title: Musk's empire as SpaceX heads to Wall Street liftoff
items.summary: NEW YORK: Elon Musk defines himself as an engineer trying to save humanity, while his critics call him a far-right showman who overpromises and underdelivers. - SpaceX, Starlink, xAI - SpaceX builds rockets and spacecraft, and has become the dominant force in commercial space launches — regularly sending astronauts to the International Space Station and satellites into orbit. Its Starlink division beams high-speed internet from space to users around the world.

items.title: US border wall offers Malaysia a cautionary lesson on security strategy
items.summary: TUCSON: Illegal crossings into the United States along parts of Arizona's border with Mexico have fallen sharply, but a senior congressional aide said the decline was not due to the border wall alone. Karamargin said Arizona's experience offered practical lessons for countries considering border walls, including Malaysia. The US has the Customs and Border Protection (CBP) agency, while Malaysia has the Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA).

items.title: Iran weighs US offer as Trump says peace talks on 'borderline'
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran said on Wednesday it was examining a new US proposal to end the Middle East war, as President Donald Trump described the talks as being on the "borderline" between a deal and renewed strikes. Trump, who said earlier that negotiations were in their "final stages", later warned that the window for diplomacy could close quickly. Ghalibaf warned of a "forceful response", while Iran's Revolutionary Guards said any renewed conflict would spread far beyond the Middle East.

items.title: Ex-American Express Malaysia staff in 'bully boss' case hauls HQ to court
items.summary: Kuhendran, who joined the company in 2014 before his dismissal in 2022, has now filed a civil suit against American Express (Malaysia), its headquarters in New York, as well as several former managers and employees. In last week's Industrial Court decision, the court found that Kuhendran had been subjected to a hostile work environment and repeatedly bullied by Vijay. Kuhendran further alleged that a separate independent investigation commissioned by the company's headquarters was concealed during Industrial Court proceedings linked to his dismissal.

items.title: Strong EPF earnings may not guarantee bigger dividends, experts caution [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Economists have cautioned Employees Provident Fund contributors against expecting sharply higher dividends despite the fund's strong first-quarter performance. Universiti Putra Malaysia School of Business and Economics lecturer Associate Professor Dr Lee Chin said that EPF determines dividends based on realised and distributable income rather than unrealised paper gains from market rallies. EPF LIKELY TO STAY CAUTIOUS Lee said global market uncertainties and geopolitical risks could still affect investment performance in the coming quarters, making it important for EPF to maintain a balanced and sustainable dividend approach.

items.title: Understanding Malaysia’s many school systems and how they coexist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — From national schools and vernacular streams to tahfiz institutions, Chinese independent schools, international campuses and alternative learning systems, Malaysia’s education landscape reflects the country’s multicultural roots and evolving social priorities. The national school system Malaysia’s largest education stream remains the government school system under the Ministry of Education, comprising Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) and Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK). Today, tahfiz education exists in multiple forms, including government-supported tahfiz schools, integrated tahfiz programmes, private tahfiz schools and independent religious schools.

items.title: 'I'm not greedy': January 6 rioters and Trump allies eye US$1.8bil 'weaponisation' fund
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Since President Donald Trump's administration announced the creation of a US$1.776 billion fund for Americans deemed to be victims of political "weaponisation," January 6 Capitol riot defendants and other Trump allies have scrambled to figure out how to get their share. "I'm not greedy," Tarrio said. Trump pardoned more than 1,500 January 6 defendants last year.

items.title: Injured Bornean elephant spotted in Kalabakan
items.summary: A BORNEAN elephant has been spotted here recently with injuries to its trunk. Sabah Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jaffry Ariffin said the sighting came amid heightened scrutiny of the species after a male elephant was found with its tusks removed in Tongod. The elephant with apparent injuries to its trunk was captured on video, which has gone viral.

items.title: Gamuda Aussie-unit bags RM3.12b contract from Edify Energy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Gamuda Bhd, through its wholly owned Australian subsidiary DT Infrastructure Pty Ltd, has secured a RM3.12 billion contract from Edify Energy Pty Ltd to deliver the Smoky Creek and Guthrie's Gap Solar Power Stations in Central Queensland, Australia. The developments are set to become the largest solar and battery hybrid projects currently under construction in Australia, marking a key milestone in the country's renewable energy transition. Together, the projects will feature a combined solar generation capacity of 720 megawatts (MW), supported by a 600MW/2,400 megawatt-hour battery energy storage system, enabling large-scale renewable power generation and improved grid stability through energy storage capabilities.

items.title: US charges former Cuban president with murder as pressure builds
items.summary: MIAMI: The United States on Wednesday indicted Cuba's former leader Raul Castro on murder charges, fuelling speculation that President Donald Trump will try to topple the communist state. Castro is the younger brother of Fidel Castro, the late iconic US nemesis who led Cuba's 1959 communist revolution. The US government previously seized on a domestic indictment to justify military action in January that toppled and seized Venezuelan president Nicolas Maduro, a staunch ally of Cuba.

items.title: US lifts sanctions on UN expert on Palestinians
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States on Wednesday removed sanctions on Francesca Albanese, a UN expert on the Palestinian territories who has harshly criticised Israel, following a court order. US District Judge Richard Leon last week granted a preliminary injunction against the sanctions. Albanese denies allegations of antisemitism, which have also been made by Israel.

items.title: WHO chief: If 'Malaysia Boleh!' other countries can, too
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The iconic "Malaysia Boleh" slogan was featured in a speech by World Health Organisation (WHO) director-general Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. He said other nations should emulate the spirit of "Malaysia Boleh" to achieve health sovereignty and equity. Speaking at the 79th World Health Assembly in Geneva, Dr Tedros said he learnt the phrase, which means "Malaysia can", when he met Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim during his visit here earlier this year.

items.title: Speaker confirms Rafizi and Nik Nazmi have resigned as MPs, EC informed of Pandan and Setiawangsa vacancies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul has confirmed receiving resignation notices from Datuk Seri Mohd Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad respectively as Members of Parliament for Pandan and Setiawangsa, effective May 18. On Monday, Rafizi, who is former PKR deputy president, and Nik Nazmi, the party’s former vice-president, also announced their departure from the party through letters addressed to the party secretary-general. They subsequently took over a political party known as Parti Bersama Malaysia (BERSAMA), a small political party based in Penang.

items.title: Federal Court affirms 30-year sentence for ex-Rela member who shot dead friend at Terengganu temple
items.summary: A three-judge panel chaired by Datuk Rhodzariah Bujang unanimously dismissed the appeal of Ong Chow Lee, 62, to set aside the murder conviction decided by the Court of Appeal on July 2, 2025. “The court finds that the appellant’s appeal has no merit. The Court of Appeal on July 2, 2025 dismissed the appellant’s appeal against the conviction and sentence and confirmed the previous decision of the High Court.

items.title: Transport Ministry: AirBorneo may apply to operate three Sarawak intercity routes commercially
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — AirBorneo may submit applications to provide flight services for the Kuching-Sibu, Kuching-Miri and Kuching-Bintulu routes on a commercial basis as the routes are not categorised as Rural Air Services (RAS). “Therefore, flights on these routes should not receive subsidies as they involve two cities, similar to any inter-city flight in Peninsular Malaysia. “The change in status of these routes to commercial routes will further encourage the participation of local airlines to operate and subsequently provide air travellers in rural Sabah and Sarawak with more flight options,” the ministry said in a statement today.

items.title: Selangor Raja Muda confers state medals on 225 recipients in conjunction with Sultan’s 80th birthday
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 20 — The Raja Muda of Selangor, Tengku Amir Shah, today conferred Selangor state medals on 225 recipients in conjunction with the 80th birthday celebration of the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah. During the investiture ceremony at the Dewan Jubli Perak Sultan Abdul Aziz here, a total of 43 individuals received the Pingat Pekerti Terpilih (P.P.T), while 35 recipients were awarded the Pingat Perkhidmatan Cemerlang Selangor (P.P.C.). ), while 13 recipients received the Pingat Perkhidmatan Selangor (P.P.S.).

items.title: Sabah chief minister says rural water projects to benefit from RM4b federal allocation
items.summary: TONGOD, May 20 — Over RM4 billion in allocations has been channeled by the Federal Government to Sabah to implement Rural Water Supply (BALB) projects, which will help address the water issues faced by rural residents. Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said that the authority to implement these projects has also been devolved to the state government, and this year, over RM4 billion was channelled by the Ministry of Rural and Regional Development (KKDW) for this purpose. “Last April, the Federal Government through KKDW handed over the authority to the state government to implement Water Supply projects with an allocation of over RM4 billion.

items.title: Repatriation of elephants from Japan requires clear evidence of welfare breaches, says Taiping council
items.summary: IPOH, May 20 — The Taiping Municipal Council (MPT) has stressed that any allegations relating to the neglect of elephant welfare at Tennoji Zoo in Japan must be supported by reasonable evidence before any move to terminate the cooperation programme or initiate the repatriation process can be considered. MPT president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan said decisions concerning the termination of the programme could not be made unilaterally as they are subject to the terms and provisions agreed upon in the agreement between the parties involved. Mohamed Akmal said the zoo is responsible for ensuring that the elephants’ welfare, health, nutrition, veterinary treatment, safety and daily management are carried out in accordance with international standards and best practices.

items.title: ECRL training programme in China building Malaysia’s future rail workforce, says Loke
items.summary: LIUZHOU (China), May 20 — The East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) Industrial Skills Training Programme (PLKI-ECRL), conducted at Liuzhou Railway Vocational Technical College (LRVTC), is helping Malaysia develop a skilled workforce for the rail industry. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said students under the year-long operations and maintenance (O&M) programme are being exposed to China’s advanced rail expertise and technical capabilities. Loke also encouraged Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions in Malaysia to collaborate with LRVTC to help produce more skilled talent for the railway sector.

items.title: First batch of Malaysian ECRL trainees graduate in China ahead of 2027 rail launch
items.summary: LIUZHOU (China), May 20 — A total of 66 Malaysian trainees graduated from Liuzhou Railway Vocational Technical College (LRVTC) on Tuesday, as part of the Batch 1 East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) Industrial Skills Training Programme (PLKI-ECRL) for Operations and Maintenance (O&M) Training. The PLKI-ECRL programme is owned by Malaysia Rail Link Sdn Bhd (MRL) and China Communications Construction ECRL Sdn Bhd. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the graduates will be absorbed into the ECRL workforce, marking an important transition from trainees to professionals in Malaysia’s railway industry.

items.title: Police recover ‘Rambo’ knife allegedly used in Alor Setar stabbing case
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 20 — Police have recovered a survival-style combat knife believed to have been used by a 32-year-old suspect in the stabbing of a woman in the neck inside a car in front of a restaurant along Jalan Titi Gajah near here on Sunday, in a case linked to jealousy and unrequited love. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the sharp weapon, commonly referred to locally as a “Rambo knife”, was found last Monday about 200 metres from the scene in bushes near a paddy field area. Meanwhile, Adzli said the police would dispose of prohibited substances at the Bukit Pelanduk Solid Waste Disposal Centre in Negeri Sembilan, involving various types of drugs and prohibited substances worth an estimated RM12.39 million that had completed legal proceedings and received court disposal orders.

items.title: Sarawak plans own space agency and aims to launch first nanosatellite by 2030, says Abang Johari
items.summary: KUCHING, May 20 — Sarawak is planning to establish its own space agency, said Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg. “Apart from that, the Sarawak Aerospace Advisory Council (SAAC) has been established to provide strategic direction and comprehensive guidance in driving the establishment of Sarawak’s aerospace sector, in line with current global industry standards,” he said when winding up the debate on the Sarawak Premier’s Department on the motion of thanks to the Yang Dipertua Negeri at the Sarawak State Legislative Assembly here today. Abang Johari said Sarawak is also targeting the launch of Sarawak’s first nanosatellite by 2030, through CubeSat-based technology which involves the use of small satellites for real-time data and earth observation.

items.title: ‘Anything is possible’: Negeri Sembilan MB does not rule out state election this year
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 20 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has not ruled out the possibility of the state election being held this year, although the matter is still under consideration. The Negeri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman said the possibility of holding the election simultaneously with neighbouring states Melaka and Johor would also be looked into. “The possibility is there (for a state election).

items.title: Federal Court cuts ex-university student’s cannabis sentence from 30 years to 12
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — The Federal Court today reduced the prison sentence of a former private university student from 30 years to 12 years and 10 strokes of the cane after he was found in possession of 1,900.9 grammes of cannabis six years ago. A three-judge panel chaired by Justice Rhodzariah Bujang unanimously allowed the final appeal by Muhammad Nur Arif Nur Akil, 28, to set aside the Court of Appeal’s conviction and sentence delivered on May 26, 2025, and substitute it with a conviction for possession. However, the Court of Appeal on May 26, 2025, upheld the High Court’s decision after dismissing his appeal.

items.title: Bintulu Port transfer to be finalised by end June, says Uggah
items.summary: KUCHING, May 20 — The handing over of Bintulu Port to Sarawak will be finalised by the end of June this year, said Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas. He said the federal government has agreed to the transfer of Bintulu Port to Sarawak government for a value of RM1.8 billion. In other developments, Uggah who is also Sarawak infrastructure and port development minister, said as of April this year, Sarawak-Sabah Link Road (SSLR) Phase 1 has achieved 70.05 percent physical progress and is expected to be completed in 2027.

items.title: Federal Court maintains 30-year sentence, 16 strokes for Ukrainian over 24kg meth haul
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — The Federal Court here today upheld the 30-year prison sentence and 16 strokes of the cane imposed on a Ukrainian national for trafficking 24.753 kg of methamphetamine four years ago. A three-member bench comprising Justices Datuk Rhodzariah Bujang, Datuk Lee Swee Seng and Datuk Ravinthran N. Paramaguru dismissed the appeal filed by Akahimo (also known as Akanimo) against his conviction and sentence. On June 26, 2024, the High Court found Akahimo guilty and sentenced him to 30 years in prison with 16 strokes of the cane.

items.title: RMAF confirms F/A-18D Hornet lands safely at KKIA after engine trouble mid-flight
items.summary: LABUAN, May 20 — The Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) has confirmed that an F/A-18D Hornet aircraft, deployed on a detachment at the Labuan Air Base for training purposes, experienced an engine issue during its return flight after undergoing a night Air-to-Surface Firing Exercise in Kota Belud, Sabah, yesterday. “The aircraft landed safely at 7:47pm,” the statement read. Following the incident, the RMAF deployed a technical team to KKIA to conduct inspections and provide technical assistance for the aircraft.

items.title: Johor exco vows action after MBJB contractors caught on CCTV running over dog
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 19 — The Johor Bahru City Council (MBJB) will take action after identifying contractors involved in deliberately running over and killing a dog in a pick-up truck here last week. The incident, which was captured on closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage, has since triggered public outrage against a team of MBJB contractors during a stray dog eradication operation in Puteri Wangsa here on May 15. Johor Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor said MBJB’s internal investigation had identified the individuals involved as appointed contractors carrying out the stray dog eradication operation.

items.title: Man pleads not guilty in Kedah to using other individuals’ MyKad numbers to buy subsidised petrol
items.summary: BALING, May 20 — A man pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here today to 13 charges of using other people’s identity card numbers to purchase Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) petrol in this district. Azman Aziz, 53, was accused of impersonating others by using the identity card numbers of three individuals to purchase 594.459 litres of subsidised petrol under the Budi Madani scheme, causing the complainants to lose their monthly subsidised fuel quotas. Sessions Court Judge Rohaida Ishak, acting as Magistrate, allowed the accused bail of RM15,000 and set July 8 for case mention.

items.title: Sabah Customs foils drug smuggling attempt worth over RM3.1m involving courier parcels and airport luggage
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 20 — The Royal Malaysian Customs Department of Sabah successfully foiled attempts to distribute various types of drugs worth over RM3,153,650 through three separate seizures at the Tawau International Airport and the Pos Aviation Warehouse in Kota Kinabalu recently. Sabah State Customs Director, Dr Ahmad Taufik Sulaiman, said that at around 1.35pm on May 11, officers on duty inspected luggage at the Tawau Airport after scanning images detected suspicious contents inside four bags at the Pos Aviation Warehouse. “In the second case, the Sabah JKDM Narcotics Branch seized a package suspected to contain 4,678 grammes of methamphetamine worth RM233,900 at the Pos Aviation Warehouse in Kota Kinabalu on May 11.

items.title: Simen Rahmah scheme continues with 1.6 million tonnes cement allocation for affordable housing, says minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — The Simen Rahmah Scheme will continue this year with 1.6 million metric tonnes of cement allocated to support the target of building 500,000 affordable housing units under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP). Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the scheme, which offers cement at prices lower than the market rate, has also been expanded to cover affordable housing projects under the Federal Government, state governments and the private sector. “The first phase of the Simen Rahmah initiative will provide 1.6 million metric tonnes of cement to support affordable housing projects priced at RM300,000 and below per unit.

items.title: Fahmi: MyKad-based age verification already standard practice in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the use of official documents such as MyKad for age verification is already a common practice in Malaysia and is not inconsistent with existing digital verification systems. He said identity verification using official documents is widely adopted across various sectors, particularly in e-wallet services and other digital platforms. He also said the Personal Data Protection Act would apply to the handling and management of personal data throughout such verification processes.

items.title: Two police reports lodged over claims on Taman Segar housing status change, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — Two police reports have been lodged against allegations that houses in Taman Segar, Cheras had been changed from leasehold to freehold, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said. She described the allegations, which also claimed that an announcement was made about the matter at a media conference in Taman Segar regarding the gazettement of a field for public use, as slander because such an announcement was never made. “Police reports have been lodged, two police reports.

items.title: DBKL to offer up to three-year business licence renewals from July 1, says FT minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall’s (DBKL) initiative to introduce business licence renewals of up to three years beginning July 1 is expected to benefit nearly 95,000 licence holders while strengthening a more business-friendly and efficient urban business ecosystem. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the initiative was introduced to ease the burden of annual licence renewals, which often place pressure on traders, especially towards the end of the year. Hannah said DBKL would offer a five per cent discount on licence fees for two-year renewals and 10 per cent for three-year renewals.

items.title: Sarawak projects RM13.04b revenue in 2026, says deputy premier
items.summary: KUCHING, May 20 — Sarawak is projecting a revenue of RM13.04 billion to be collected this year, said Sarawak Deputy Premier and Second Minister for Finance and New Economy Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas. Uggah said that as of April, RM3.86 billion, or 29.6 per cent of the projected amount, has been collected. Uggah said given the prevailing economic headwinds and geopolitical uncertainties affecting global oil and gas markets, the state government foresee an impact on this year’s revenue projection.

items.title: New trains coming in 2031: Rapid Rail plans 26-unit Kelana Jaya LRT fleet upgrade to boost capacity and reduce disruptions
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 20 — A total of 26 new trains for the Kelana Jaya Light Rail Transit (LRT) Line are expected to begin operating in 2031 in an effort to increase capacity and operational efficiency as well as reduce service disruptions to users. LRT Rapid Rail chief operating officer, Nor Azmi Mohd Yusof said the process of replacing existing trains had begun with the first train scheduled to arrive in 2029 before the entire replacement is targeted to be completed by the end of 2030. Commenting on the increase in the number of users, Nor Azmi said the load factor for the Kelana Jaya Line had reached 109 per cent in April.

items.title: Female teacher in Seremban faces five sexual assault charges involving 14-year-old male student
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 20 — A female teacher pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court today to five counts of physical and non-physical sexual assault against a 14-year-old male student, dating back to September last year. For two counts of physical sexual assault, the class teacher allegedly committed the act under a tree outside an apartment here between Sept 12 and 20, 2025, around 2.30 pm. The accused faces an additional physical sexual assault charge under Section 377B of the Penal Code, which carries a similar penalty.

items.title: Survey shows Malaysians becoming more intolerant of differences amid rising ‘insular trust’ trend
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — More Malaysians are becoming less tolerant of people with differing opinions and values, according to the 2026 Edelman Trust Barometer, which points to a deepening “insular trust” mindset and widening social polarisation. Despite improving overall trust in institutions such as businesses and the government, the report suggests a worrying trend: Malaysians are increasingly retreating into familiar information environments and are less willing to trust those with different values, political views or cultural backgrounds. On average, 65 per cent of Malaysians now exhibit an insular trust mindset, while only 37 per cent regularly seek information from sources with differing political viewpoints, the survey found.

items.title: Pakatan secretariat to review media complaint over alleged rough treatment at convention, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said he has instructed the Pakatan Harapan (PH) Convention secretariat to review a complaint lodged by the Johor Media Club (KMJ) over an alleged rough incident involving media practitioners during the convention on Sunday. “As the director of the convention, I deeply regret the incident. On Tuesday, KMJ lodged a police report over alleged aggressive behaviour by security personnel towards media practitioners covering the 2026 PH Convention in Johor Bahru on Sunday.
